body: Würde da noch Spider und Ref in die Route reinbringen, damit's klarer ist.
Can we omit the 'connected' in the path (also on lines 27 and 41)? Would be nicer without ;-)
Should we use `:code/data` endpoint? Is a bit confusing to have same endpoint (PUT/POST) for two completely different things.
These two lines can be removed

I think we should not return CryptoInputHistory here, but also BuyCryptoHistory. CryptoInputs are just inputs. Depending on the route, either a BuyFiat, a BuyCrypto or a CryptoStaking is created. So for CryptoRoute, you'll also find the history in the BuyCrypto table.
I would have the the id of the BankAccount to be updated in the route (see e.g. BuyController).
Use this method on lines 85 and 92 as well.
Switch to a generic KycStatus/KycState update API
Use min. deposits from toDto method (extract to separate method?)
I think it would be useful to write some unit tests for this code 😉
I would add a test with a special currency (e.g. GBP) to see if it falls back to the EUR account.
Already some ideas on how to handle BSC vs. Ethereum?
Is there something missing? We're not calling the BuyCrypto and BuyFiat Service? 🤔
Params are missing in URL or switch to body?
Params are missing in URL or switch to body?
Params are missing in URL or switch to body?
Environment `test` does not exist. It's `loc`, `dev` or `prd`.
Did you test the code? 😉  I nowhere see a mapping from filterCols (in DTO) to customCols in interface.
This logic is shared with KycWebhookService. Maybe we can share it somehow?
I think we need an explicit mapping here. Otherwise the API will return all fields of the wallet (I think).
SpiderData can be included in the original SQL query I think?
At DFX we use camelCase for URLs (doesn't really make sense, but ... 😉 )
Does it make sense to have a controller for each sub-topic?
Add the same API call with JWT instead of code.
I think I would have two separate routes for old and new logic, makes it much easier to read and refactor. WDYT?
What about the legacy routes in the AdminController. Are they not removed?
We can then change all existing to the new scheme and remove the old one.
It's a bit weird to have a dedicated endpoint, but a generic service method and DTO 😉
Move this also into `userService. getUserBuyFee`, to have everything in one place?
Move this also into `userService. getUserSellFee`, to have everything in one place?
Can we move the methods `getTransactionVolume` and `transformToMinDeposit` to the config class? Would make more sense there 🤔
The query parameter should still be a list of blockchains
I think we still need to return a list of blockchains here, because this address may be used to login for multiple chains.
This will be unused (no CFP voting at DFX) => best to remove it from Swagger
Maybe we should add some DTOs here? 😉

I personally would prefer the following syntax (for all mappings):
```ts
return this.bankService.getAllBanks().then(BankDtoMapper.entitiesToDto);
```
WDYT?
I think this method should also be moved to KycService.
Path should be `:id/documents` where `id` is the user address.
No rate limit on sign in.
Remove the `enabled` from CountryDto, is not used anymore.
This line needs to be kept (or you can remove the asset field on the DTO, this route is not used anyway).

```ts
jest.spyOn(paymentInfoService, 'buyCheck').mockImplementation(async (dto) => dto);
```
I would use a `POST` request here (createBfsAddressRequest or somethiing)
To have a clean REST API, you would probably use `@Delete(':id/amlCheck')`

body: The `getUserBuyFee` method on `UserService` can then probably be removed (same for sell and crypto)?

Plain string responses are annoying in a client normally handling JSON. Additionally the client also needs the k1, to be able to fetch the result. So maybe return something like:
```ts
{
   k1: string,
   lnurl: string
}
```

Same here. Maybe return something like:
```ts
{
   isComplete: boolean,
   accessToken?: string
}
```
The client (in our case the services) doesn't know `signature` and `key`.
I think `@Put('discountCodes')` (plural) and having the code as a query param would make it clearer. And maybe rename the method to `addDiscountCode`.
I would put this endpoint under root (`@Get()`)
I think these endpoints could be made public? And maybe documented with something like `@ApiOperation({description: 'Initiate history export'})`
Can these calls here be redirected to the new controller?

body: I think we don't need any controllers

body: This is in the v2 controller, so there should be no clashes.
I think we can omit authentication here -> remove `ApiBearerAuth` and `UseGuards`

Ok, I see some issues here:
- The user will click a link he received by mail. Therefore he might not be logged in in the Services
   - We cannot have an auth guard
   - We can only return an access token, if the user came with one
   - Maybe we should remove the `redirectUrl` in the response and just return the master's KYC code and the JWT, if the user was logged in
- The User might have an "account-only" account, which means he has no user
   - In this case (there is no `user` in the JWT) use `generateAccountToken`
`You can only refund sell or swap transactions`

body: There is already a `RouteController`, but it's a bit misplaced 🤔
I think we don't need an API to create a route. We should add some code to add a route for every existing buy/sell/swap route and automatically create them for new ones.
Can't we move the existing code into the new controller?
I would probably use `:ticket-id/message/:message-id/file` as URL and omit the query
This was correct, can be reverted
Move down to ident endpoints, maybe use `@Put('ident/manual/:id')`?
Maybe it's more a `contractTransaction` instead of `rawInputData`?
Do we have to parse the `data`, or is it already parsed?
I think we want the real auth guard here (not the optional one)
IBAN endpoints can be labeled as deprecated?
I would keep it in the UserController (we don't have anything public in the UserDataController), the user doesn't know about UserData
Can't we use the new transaction history thing instead of using the Promise.all (which is probably more expensive)?
I think I would create the API key on user data, for all new keys. For existing ones, we would need to update/delete it on the user. WDYT?
Update on user only if `jwt.user` is not null
`0.02` probably should go into config (also in `buy-crypto.service.ts`)
Shouldn't this be network fee instead of dfx?
The entire controller is excluded from Swagger
Let's call it `confirmBuy()`? Would be similar to sell.
Put this into a helper method
Add `request-uid` query parameter here (just for completeness)
I would just write `throw new BadRequestException('Process disabled')` as we don't know if it really is the safety module
Maybe we should place this endpoint under root (just `custody`)

I think I would place this endpoint in `PaymentLinkController` under `GET paymentLink/recipient`.

Maybe also with query parameters instead of path parameter? I'm not sure 🤔
Maybe we can then somehow share the checks performed on the route? They are already in `PaymentLinkService`.
This sell DTO is a legacy thing that nobody uses 🤣  I would create a new one (e.g. `PaymentRecipient`) with the required information.
Use `req.body` instead of `JSON.stringify(dto)` in error message and move JSON-parsing into try-catch?
Errors should be handled (and logged) with .catch, if it's not awaited.
trim is probably not required here 😉
I would move both API endpoints to the new controller
You could combine these two with `@Get(':exportType')`, the code looks very similar
I would name everything `RealUnit` instead of `Realunit` 😉
Should we do some caching here? Or maybe in a later stage?
`getOwnRecommendationForUserData` vs `getAllRecommendationForUserData` is a bit weird. One is for recommender and one for recommended, right?
I think we don't need both APIs, we can filter in the frontend?
It should also be possible to reject
Would it make sense to return the same DTO in `price` and `price/history`? Or do we want the current price only in CHF?
Swap is actually buy+sell, or nothing? 🤔

body: Is this documentation not available yet?
I think I would place this endpoint next to the `PUT /personalIban` as `GET /personalIban`, WDYT?
`UserRole.ACCOUNT` should be enough? And we should probably do the same for `  @Post('/personalIban')`
It matches `kycFileList`, I think this is ok
try-catch is actually clearer in this case. We e.g. cannot return from the method in a `.catch`, and rethrowing is als cumbersome.

It's not: `getTransactionByUid` vs `getTransactionByRequestUid`
But we could sumarize the fallback?
I would use a general cancel endpoint ` @Delete(':id') `
Do we need to hide some data for non-compliance users?
Maybe we should use the user role for this switch?
Does it make sense to pass the data through the frontend? We can just fetch the required data when the user calls API endpoints.

Hm, I'm not sure 🤣  for the user endpoints we also have only a single PUT endpoint, DTO validation can be done conditionally.
If we want to keep two endpoints, they should at least be named and use paths so one understands what they are doing. Both do refunds, one is just specific.
